records.ety: [OE. partenen, OF. partenir, fr. L. pertinere to stretch out, reach, pertain; per + tenere to hold, keep. See Per-, and Tenable, and cf. Appertain, Pertinent.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pertained (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Pertaining.]

defs: To belong; to have connection with, or dependence on, something, as an appurtenance, attribute, etc.; to appertain; as, saltness pertains to the ocean; flowers pertain to plant life.
quotes: Men hate those who affect that honor by ambition which pertaineth not to them.

defs: To have relation or reference to something.
quotes: These words pertain unto us at this time as they pertained to them at their time.
